text:
Overview
The Microsoft Defender family delivers Extended Detection and Response (XDR) across the
modern attack surface. Individual products correlate signal into the Microsoft Defender
XDR portal for unified incident and alert triage, complementing Sentinel's broader SIEM/
SOAR role.
Product Family
Product
Protects
Defender for
Endpoint
Windows/Linux/macOS/mobile — EDR, vulnerability management,
attack surface reduction
Defender for
Identity
On-prem AD — detects lateral movement, DCSync, Kerberoasting via
domain controller sensors
Defender for Office
365
Email/collaboration — phishing, malware, Safe Links/Attachments,
campaign views
Defender for Cloud
Apps
SaaS/CASB — shadow IT discovery, session/access policies, OAuth
app governance
Defender for Cloud
Multi-cloud CSPM + CWPP — posture management and workload
protection (Azure/AWS/GCP)

protection (Azure/AWS/GCP)

Defender for Cloud — CSPM/CWPP
Secure Score — weighted, actionable posture metric across recommendations
Regulatory compliance dashboard — maps controls to CIS, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001, NIST
Workload protection plans — per-resource-type (servers, storage, SQL, containers,
Key Vault, App Service)
C L O U D C H E AT S H E E T · S E C U R I T Y
Microsoft Defender Suite
XDR portfolio spanning endpoints, identity, email, cloud apps, and cloud workloads

Page 1 of 3

Agentless scanning — VM disk and container image scanning without in-guest agents
Multi-cloud connectors — onboard AWS (via CloudFormation) and GCP (via Terraform/
console) for unified posture
Attack path analysis — graph-based visualization of exploitable chains toward crown-
jewel assets
Defender for Endpoint — Key Concepts
Device groups & tags drive RBAC and automation scoping
Attack Surface Reduction (ASR) rules block common malware/exploit techniques
(e.g., Office macro abuse)
Automated Investigation & Remediation (AIR) auto-triages and remediates low-
complexity alerts
Live Response gives responders a remote shell for forensic collection and containment
Threat & Vulnerability Management ties CVE exposure directly to device inventory
Unified Incident Correlation
Defender XDR automatically correlates alerts across endpoint, identity, email, and cloud app signals into
a single incident with a shared attack story — reducing alert fatigue versus triaging each product

a single incident with a shared attack story — reducing alert fatigue versus triaging each product
console independently. Sentinel can further ingest these as high-fidelity incidents via the Microsoft
Defender XDR connector.
Common Pitfalls
Pitfall
Fix
Running ASR rules
straight to block
mode
Pilot in audit mode first, review impacted users/apps, then enforce
Ignoring Secure
Score
recommendations as
noise
Triage by weighted risk reduction, not raw count; a few items often
move the score most
Defender for Cloud
enabled without
workload plans
Free tier is CSPM-only; enable paid plans for actual threat detection
(CWPP)
Use unified Defender XDR portal for cross-signal incident correlation

Treating each
Defender console as
independent
No automated
response to high-
confidence alerts
Wire AIR / Logic Apps playbooks for auto-containment on high-fidelity
detections
Interview / Exam Quick Hits
Defender for Cloud Free tier = CSPM recommendations only; plans (paid) add CWPP
threat detection
Defender for Identity sensors run on domain controllers/AD FS servers, not
endpoints
Secure Score recommendations are weighted by potential risk reduction, not simple
count
ASR rules can run in audit mode before enforcement to validate false-positive impact
Defender for Cloud Apps enforces real-time control via reverse proxy (Conditional
Access App Control)
